root_text.an5.132:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgato rañño cakkavattissa jeṭṭho putto pitarā pavattitaṁ cakkaṁ dhammeneva anuppavatteti;
root_text.an5.132:1.2: taṁ hoti cakkaṁ appaṭivattiyaṁ kenaci manussabhūtena paccatthikena pāṇinā.
root_text.an5.132:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño cakkavattissa jeṭṭho putto atthaññū ca hoti, dhammaññū ca, mattaññū ca, kālaññū ca, parisaññū ca.
root_text.an5.132:2.3: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi aṅgehi samannāgato rañño cakkavattissa jeṭṭho putto pitarā pavattitaṁ cakkaṁ dhammeneva anuppavatteti;
root_text.an5.132:2.4: taṁ hoti cakkaṁ appaṭivattiyaṁ kenaci manussabhūtena paccatthikena pāṇinā.
root_text.an5.132:3.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato sāriputto tathāgatena anuttaraṁ dhammacakkaṁ pavattitaṁ sammadeva anuppavatteti;
root_text.an5.132:3.2: taṁ hoti cakkaṁ appaṭivattiyaṁ samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.132:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, sāriputto atthaññū, dhammaññū, mattaññū, kālaññū, parisaññū.
root_text.an5.132:4.3: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato sāriputto tathāgatena anuttaraṁ dhammacakkaṁ pavattitaṁ sammadeva anuppavatteti;
root_text.an5.132:4.4: taṁ hoti cakkaṁ appaṭivattiyaṁ samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an5.132:1.1: “Mendicants, possessing five factors a wheel-turning monarch’s eldest son continues to wield the power set in motion by his father only in a principled manner.
translation_text.an5.132:1.2: And this power cannot be undermined by any human enemy.
translation_text.an5.132:2.2: A wheel-turning monarch’s oldest son knows what is right, knows principle, knows moderation, knows the right time, and knows the assembly.
translation_text.an5.132:2.3: A wheel-turning monarch’s oldest son who possesses these five factors continues to wield the power set in motion by his father only in a principled manner.
translation_text.an5.132:2.4: And this power cannot be undermined by any human enemy.
translation_text.an5.132:3.1: In the same way, possessing five qualities Sāriputta rightly keeps rolling the supreme Wheel of Dhamma that was rolled forth by the Realized One.
translation_text.an5.132:3.2: And that wheel cannot be turned back by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or divinity or by anyone in the world.
translation_text.an5.132:4.2: Sāriputta knows what is right, knows principle, knows moderation, knows the right time, and knows the assembly.
translation_text.an5.132:4.3: Possessing these five qualities Sāriputta rightly keeps rolling the supreme Wheel of Dhamma that was rolled forth by the Realized One.
translation_text.an5.132:4.4: And that wheel cannot be turned back by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or divinity or by anyone in the world.”
